# Demo Article: The Moon

The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite. It reflects sunlight and appears to
change shape during a repeating cycle of phases. Those phases are caused by the
Moon's position relative to Earth and the Sun, not by Earth's shadow except
during a lunar eclipse.

The Moon affects Earth in practical ways. Its gravity helps drive ocean tides,
and its regular motion has been used for calendars, navigation, and night-sky
observation. Robotic spacecraft and crewed missions have also used the Moon as
a nearby destination for studying planetary geology.

This fixture is intentionally harmless. It is synthetic article-style text for
local governance testing, not a copied encyclopedia article or regulated data.
